content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the pending br safety remediation additively: `br` health checks now distinguish real line-level merge conflicts from ordinary JSON content, preserve non-`NotFound` read failures as `IoError`, and expose shared operator-facing failure details; milestone bead mutations, CLI bead claiming, and success reconciliation now all run the same preflight health guard before `br update`/`br close` flows; `BrAdapter::check_available()` was relaxed to treat a launchable `br` binary as available even if `--version` exits nonzero so existing fixture scripts remain valid; and regression coverage was expanded for the new health classifications plus the new claim/reconciliation safety guards. I also updated the CLI integration workspace fixture to materialize a healthy `.beads/issues.jsonl` by default so the new safety rules apply without breaking unrelated bead-backed project tests.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the existing `br` health check implementation and every bead mutation entrypoint called out by the amendments to determine where shared preflight validation had to be enforced.
2. [Completed] Harden `src/adapters/br_health.rs` by making conflict detection line-based, adding an `IoError` classification for unreadable exports, and centralizing operator-facing failure text for blocked bead mutations.
3. [Completed] Wire the shared bead-health preflight into `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`, `src/cli/project.rs`, and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs`, then update unit/integration tests and fixtures so the new guards are covered without restructuring existing flows.
4. [Intended] Run the full validation gates required by the bead: `cargo fmt --check`, targeted regressions, `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, and the final `nix build` on the finished tree.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` exited 0 after formatting the new changes.
- Targeted regressions passed: `cargo test check_beads_health -- --nocapture`, `cargo test claim_bead_in_br -- --nocapture`, and `cargo test reconcile_success_rejects_conflicted_beads_before_close -- --nocapture`.
- `cargo test` exited 0 on the full suite after updating the CLI workspace fixture for the new bead-health guard.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` exited 0.
- `nix build` was launched on the final tree and began building `/nix/store/81ykjmjpanhbnah42vsaw197ch7m5a5n-ralph-burning-static-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl-0.1.0.drv`, but no final success/failure exit was observed before the session ended.

## Outstanding Risks

- `nix build` was started against the final tree and reached the target derivation build, but it did not return a final exit code within this session, so the repository's authoritative Nix gate is still unresolved.
- The worktree still contains live orchestration-state edits under `.ralph-burning/projects/9ni44-1/`; I left them untouched per scope constraints.
